OpenSlide is an open-source suite of tools written in C for reading, processing, and converting whole-slide pathology images (WSI) in various formats like SVS and TIFF.

The software is used for Objective 1: Build a unified latent space for cells, spatial spots and WSIs - specifically for preprocessing and handling gigapixel whole-slide histopathology images before they are fed into the pathology foundation model training pipeline.

checks.name: Project & Usage Alignment
checks.notes:
The Cancer Genome Atlas (TCGA) is a large-scale, multi-institutional cancer genomics programme jointly established by the National Cancer Institute (NCI) and the National Human Genome Research Institute (NHGRI). It provides whole-slide pathology images (H&E-stained SVS/TIFF files), matched genomic profiles (DNA-seq, copy-number variations), transcriptomic data (RNA-seq, miRNA-seq), proteomics (RPPA), clinical annotations, and molecular subtyping information spanning 33 cancer types and more than 20,000 patient samples. The project requests the full open-access tier, comprising approximately 35–40 TB across all 33 sub-cohorts (TCGA-BRCA through TCGA-CHOL), to be retrieved via the GDC Data Transfer Tool using per-cohort manifest files.

The dataset serves both proposal objectives. For "Build a unified latent space for cells, spatial spots and WSIs", the whole-slide images and paired transcriptomic profiles provide the primary pretraining corpus for the pathology image encoder and the cross-modal alignment model that couples morphological patterns to cellular and molecular states. For "Demonstrate clinical utility in diagnosis, risk stratification and therapy prioritization", the clinical annotations and molecular subtyping labels across 33 cancer types provide the evaluation benchmarks for standardised grading, subtype classification, survival stratification, and therapy-response prediction tasks.

Aligns with the approved research topic, Bioscience.

Day-to-day data management and access is operated by the Genomic Data Commons (GDC) at the Center for Translational Data Science, University of Chicago. As a large federal research consortium, no individual LinkedIn profiles are applicable; institutional documentation is available at https://portal.gdc.cancer.gov/ and https://www.genome.gov/.

Non-D5 country affiliation. The owning and maintaining entities are U.S. federal government agencies and a U.S. academic institution.

checks.name: Project & Usage Alignment
checks.notes:
CZ CELLxGENE Discover Census is a large-scale single-cell transcriptomics repository maintained by the Chan Zuckerberg Initiative (CZI) Single-Cell Biology Programme. It aggregates standardised single-cell RNA-seq count matrices from hundreds of publicly available studies, currently encompassing tens of millions of human and mouse cells measured across more than 60,000 genes, with harmonised cell metadata (cell type, tissue of origin, sequencing technology, donor ID) and GENCODE-based gene metadata. The project requests the latest release (census_version="2025-11-08"), to be accessed via the cellxgene-census Python API and downloaded in h5ad format (approximately 5 TB).

The dataset serves the proposal objective "Build a unified latent space for cells, spatial spots and WSIs" by providing the pretraining corpus for the modality-specific single-cell RNA-seq encoder. The breadth of cell types, tissues, and sequencing technologies across hundreds of studies is specifically required for the cross-cohort generalisation and contrastive alignment objectives described in the proposal.

Aligns with the approved research topic, Bioscience.

checks.name: Project & Usage Alignment
checks.notes:
SpeciesLLM is a transformer-based single-cell foundation model developed in-house by the Gao Lab at KAUST. It is pretrained on large-scale single-cell transcriptomic data using masked-token or permutation-invariant objectives to learn general-purpose cell and gene embeddings across multiple species. The model is not publicly released; it resides on the lab's Ibex project storage (/ibex/project/c2307/codes/SpeciesLLM).

The model serves the proposal objective "Build a unified latent space for cells, spatial spots and WSIs" as the primary in-house single-cell foundation model. On Shaheen III GPUP it will undergo continued pretraining and fine-tuning (SFT, full FT, and PEFT) to align cell-state embeddings with pathology image representations via the cross-modal contrastive training pipeline.

Aligns with the approved research topic, Health and Wellness.

checks.name: Project & Usage Alignment
checks.notes:
scGPT is a generative foundation model for single-cell biology based on the Transformer architecture. It uses an autoregressive generative objective with specialised attention masks that respect the unordered nature of gene sets, and is pretrained on millions of cells from diverse tissues and conditions drawn from CZ CELLxGENE. It supports a range of downstream tasks including cell-type annotation, batch integration, and perturbation prediction after fine-tuning.

The model serves the proposal objective "Build a unified latent space for cells, spatial spots and WSIs" as a publicly available single-cell foundation model checkpoint for fine-tuning and alignment with pathology image encoders. On Shaheen III GPUP the declared training objectives are PEFT (LoRA) or Full Fine-Tuning for cross-modal contrastive alignment.

Aligns with the approved research topic, Health and Wellness.

checks.notes:
Geneformer (V2) is a transformer-based single-cell foundation model pretrained on large-scale single-cell transcriptomic data using masked-token objectives to learn general-purpose cell embeddings. It is designed for transfer learning across a range of tasks including cell-type classification, network biology inference, and perturbation prediction.

The model serves the proposal objective "Build a unified latent space for cells, spatial spots and WSIs" as an additional single-cell encoder backbone for cross-modal contrastive training. On Shaheen III GPUP the declared training objectives are PEFT or Full Fine-Tuning for cross-modal contrastive alignment.

Aligns with the approved research topic, Health and Wellness.

The model is listed in the Model Readiness form with the declared Shaheen III use case described as "Suitable for the 'Tongue Diagnosis Agent' or any task requiring high-fidelity categorical priors from medical images." The reference to a "Tongue Diagnosis Agent" is not mentioned anywhere in the proposal text or in the two declared project objectives. The connection to this proposal's scope (cancer pathology, WSI analysis, single-cell transcriptomics) is not established in the submission.

Aligns with the approved research topic, Health and Wellness (conditionally — pending clarification of the tongue diagnosis use case).

models.name: TRIDENT (+ bundled encoders: UNI, UNI2-h, CONCH, conchv1_5, TITAN, madeleine, abmil)

checks.name: Project & Usage Alignment
checks.notes:
TRIDENT is a scalable toolkit developed by the Mahmood Lab (Harvard Medical School) for high-throughput whole-slide image (WSI) processing. It uses vision encoders — typically hierarchical image pyramids or Vision Transformers — to partition gigapixel slides into manageable patch tokens and extract feature embeddings. The PI has declared that TRIDENT will be used together with several of the Mahmood Lab's vision encoder checkpoints: UNI, UNI2-h, CONCH, conchv1_5, TITAN, madeleine, and abmil.base.conch_v15.pc108-24k.

The toolkit and its bundled encoders serve the proposal objective "Build a unified latent space for cells, spatial spots and WSIs" as the WSI preprocessing and patch-embedding backbone. On Shaheen III GPUP the declared objectives are: (1) distributed high-throughput inference to extract patch-level embeddings from thousands of gigapixel WSIs at scale; and (2) Full FT or PEFT on advanced vision encoders (TITAN, UNI) as part of the cross-modal contrastive training pipeline.

Aligns with the approved research topic, Health and Wellness.
